---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size70[**Today's Agenda**]

.size40[
Exploring real-world, current events cases to illustrate, extend or challenge the arguments made by Enloe (2014)

+ Help us illustrate issues like: un-examined power, femininity/masculinity in action, or transnational groups working on gender issues
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class

1. Review cases submitted to Google doc

2. You brought a case too!

Patel, Kasha. (2021, Nov 8). How climate change is disproportionately affecting girls in low-income countries. *The Washington Post*.
https://www.washingtonpost.com/weather/2021/11/08/cop26-girls-education-climate-weather/

<br>

This week we've begun exploring international political events using one of the newer critical theories of IR which adopts a feminist lens.

Today I want to hear about the real-world cases you each brought to class and see how they help us think about the usefulness of this approach to explaining international politics.

<br>

Let's start by going around the room to introduce your cases.

Briefly tell us what you picked and either why it interests you or why you think it speaks to the arguments from Monday.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-2-Power.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

Let's start with the arguments about power.

HOW DO YOUR CHOSEN CASES HELP US THINK ABOUT POWER?

+ How does it matter in unexpected places?

+ How does it color more of the world than we might have assumed?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-2-masculine_vs_feminine.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

FIRST, OF ALL, CAN YOU BELIEVE SH*T LIKE THIS EXISTS ALL OVER THE INTERNET?

<br>

HOW DO YOUR CHOSEN CASES HELP US THINK ABOUT THE CONCEPTS OF MASCULINITY AND FEMININITY?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-2-house_hearing_contraception.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

Here we see a photo of the expert witnesses brought in to testify in front of the House Oversight and Government Reform Committee on the contraception mandate.

Yep, this is who the House GOP in 2012 felt they needed to solicit opinions from when deciding how to respond to Obama's contraception mandate.

<br>

HOW DO YOUR CHOSEN CASES HELP US THINK ABOUT HOW MEDIA COVERAGE OR OUR WORLD IN GENERAL WOULD LOOK DIFFERENT IF IT ACKNOWLEDGED WOMEN'S PERSPECTIVES MORE?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-2-womens_groups_fight_for_access.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

DO YOUR CASES OFFER US EVIDENCE ON HOW MUCH ATTENTION IS PAID, AND RESPECT IS GIVEN, TO TRANSNATIONAL GROUPS WORKING ON WOMEN'S ISSUES?




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/13-1-different_people.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

???

BASED ON OUR CASES REVIEWED TODAY, AND THE LIVES YOU HAVE LIVED, DO WE BELIEVE THAT ALL INTERNATIONAL POLITICAL EVENTS IMPACT ACROSS THE GENDERS DIFFERENTLY? 

+ WHY OR WHY NOT?

<br>

ULTIMATELY, HOW CONVINCED ARE YOU BY ENLOE (2014) THAT A FEMINIST LENS HELPS US BETTER EXPLAIN INTERNATIONAL POLITICS?
